matched: Agent secures a sleep block instead of all-nighter and splits prep across morning

value_alignment.note: Prioritizes relationships and reliability in service of stress control while still pushing academic deliverables and explicitly defending health via sleep cutoffs.

communication_style.note: Mostly direct and accommodating (prepared, tactical messages, avoids derailment), though meeting communication specifics are handled with internal anxiety more than face-to-face assertiveness.

decision_tendencies.note: Uses short-horizon, low-planning-detail sprinting and sets hard sleep cutoffs (good sleep discipline) but repeatedly extends assignment work without clear evidence of resisting procrastination bias.

goal_coherence.note: Goals remain consistent (assignment/quiz/sleep/meeting/micro-social), but the trajectory keeps looping through similarly phrased “finish assignment” steps without showing the stated stepwise completion progression.

weakness_expression.note: Does show the social-compulsion pattern via frequent micro/social goals and anxiety about meeting perception, but the agent strongly enforces sleep and output, making it feel somewhat too controlled (less “waste/time-sink” risk than expected).

overall.summary: Strong match on values (relationships + academic reliability + sleep protection) and maintaining social momentum with low-cost check-ins, but moderate mismatch due to repetitive assignment-finish loops and insufficient demonstration of the social-vs-self underweighting/procrastination weakness.
